content.subject: architect: design merge-guard end-to-end fix for #677 PR #1
content.description:
CONTEXT
PR #1 of #677 umbrella. Worktree: /Users/mj/Sites/collab/PACT-prompt/.worktrees/fix/677-merge-guard-end-to-end. Branch: fix/677-merge-guard-end-to-end. Variety 11 (High); ARCHITECT hard-gate locked (Scope 4, Risk 3, total 11).

Preparer task #9 — read via `TaskGet` for HANDOFF cross-references and prep doc location.
Prep doc: `docs/preparation/677-merge-guard-end-to-end.md` (in worktree, gitignored, do NOT paste content into your task descriptions — read once via Read).

MISSION
Produce architecture doc at `docs/architecture/677-merge-guard-end-to-end.md` (in worktree) that lets coders implement without re-deciding. Specifically:

1. **Resolve the 4 open questions surfaced by preparer**:
   - Q-A (Item 6 scope): Python-only / hybrid (Python + .md excluding docs/runbooks) / all-source. Choose with rationale.
   - Q-B (Item 6 enforcement): soft-warn (echo-but-allow) / deny (block commit). Choose with rationale, citing CLAUDE.md L121 audit anchor wording.
   - Q-C (Item 6 detection): docstrings/comments only / string-literals + identifiers + comments. Choose with rationale.
   - Q-D (Item 7 pin retirement): single new pin / retire-both / leave-both-with-updated-STALE-markers. Choose with rationale citing 'no backward compat for PACT' feedback memory.

2. **Choose #665 regex fix option**: preparer documented 3 (a/b/c). Choose one with rationale + provide the exact replacement regex pattern. The umbrella also says "or use a stricter parse that locates the subcommand and reads its first positional arg" — evaluate that as 4th option.

3. **Design git_commit_check.py planning-artifacts integration**: per Q-A/B/C choices, specify (a) where in git_commit_check.py the new check inserts (function name + line range), (b) the regex pattern + character class details, (c) the suppress mechanism (if any — preparer surfaced this as MEDIUM uncertainty), (d) test cases needed (positive + negative).

4. **Specify commit sequence**: this is a multi-fix PR. Design atomic commits. Suggested ordering (lead can re-order):
   - Commit 1: rename tool_output → tool_response in merge_guard_post.py + 4 docstring drift sites
   - Commit 2: preserve task_lifecycle_gate.py:315 + add explanatory comment
   - Commit 3: regenerate 64+2 fixtures
   - Commit 4: tighten _GH_PR_NUMBER_RE per chosen option
   - Commit 5: planning-artifacts regex integration
   - Commit 6: release notes + version bump
   Each commit must build/test green independently.

5. **Release notes content**: per CLAUDE.md "Tag + GitHub release after every plugin-version bump" pin, design release notes covering Phase 1 + Phase 2 of #677. Include behavioral-change comms — operators with muscle memory of "manual merge_guard_post token write" will hit unexpected denials post-merge.

6. **Version bump**: choose patch vs minor (per `feedback_version_bump_semver` memory). Lean: this is bug fixes + small enhancement (planning-artifacts regex) → patch. Confirm with rationale.

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Submit TEACHBACK on Task #10 first (per pact-teachback skill), idle on awaiting_lead_completion. Do not begin design until lead accepts.
2. After lead accepts Task #10: claim this task (Task #11), perform design above, write `docs/architecture/677-merge-guard-end-to-end.md` in the worktree, store HANDOFF in metadata.handoff (canonical 6-field schema), idle on awaiting_lead_completion.
3. SendMessage-FIRST ordering on completion: wake-signal SendMessage to team-lead BEFORE storing HANDOFF metadata + idling.

GUIDELINES
- Work in worktree path: /Users/mj/Sites/collab/PACT-prompt/.worktrees/fix/677-merge-guard-end-to-end
- CLAUDE.md is gitignored and does not exist in worktrees — do NOT edit/create it. Pin work is orchestrator-owned post-merge.
- Use Read/Grep/Glob; no Edit/Write outside docs/architecture/.
- You may query the secretary directly via SendMessage for any historical context.
- HANDOFF canonical 6-field schema: produced/decisions/reasoning_chain/uncertainty/integration/open_questions.
- Include reasoning_chain in your HANDOFF — explain how your key decisions connect (4 open questions × multiple resolution paths each, plus regex option choice + commit sequencing).
- Important pact-architecture-patterns guidance applies (single responsibility, loose coupling).

Per dogfood directive: DO NOT mark this task completed yourself. After staging the architecture doc, write metadata.handoff, send notify SendMessage to team-lead, SET intentional_wait{reason=awaiting_lead_completion}. Idle.

Upstream: TEACHBACK Task #10. Preparer findings: Task #9 (TaskGet for HANDOFF).

produced: docs/architecture/677-merge-guard-end-to-end.md (in worktree fix/677-merge-guard-end-to-end; untracked, needs lead stage+commit)
decisions: #665 regex fix: Option (b) — introduce _GH_FLAG_TOKENS = r'(?:-\S*(?:\s+\S+)?\s+)*' as a SEPARATE constant alongside existing _GH_GLOBAL_FLAGS (which stays unchanged for DANGEROUS_PATTERNS use). _GH_PR_NUMBER_RE switches to _GH_FLAG_TOKENS + adds \b after (\d+) capture. 5-line surgical diff. Probe-validated against 13+ cases including anti-evasion (heredoc body containing fake 'gh pr merge 999'). Rejected Option 4 (token-walker function) — same probe outcomes, 5x larger surface, no security gain.

Q-A Category B scope: full git_commit_check.py extension surface — .py + .md/.markdown + .yaml/.yml + .json + .txt (lead pre-confirmed in Task #10 acceptance). Path-exclusions: docs/ (gitignored in PACT-Plugin, defensive for downstream) + CHANGELOG.md (legitimate historical issue refs).
Q-B Category B enforcement: deny (exit 2). Aligns with check_security/check_hardcoded_secrets enforcement style + CLAUDE.md L121 'returns zero hits in source files' is categorical (no soft version).

Q-C Category B detection surface: full file content scan (no AST parsing). Three reasons: (a) consistency with sibling checks in git_commit_check.py, (b) the rule is content-categorical per CLAUDE.md broad enumeration, (c) AST would arbitrarily exclude string literals which the rule names as in-scope.

Suppress mechanism: structural via negative-lookaround word boundaries — `(?<![\w])F\d+(?![\w])` rejects internal Python identifiers (f1_threshold) while accepting standalone F-row labels. NO separate suppress-comment marker (deferred per preparer LOW uncertainty — over-engineering for current scope).

Q-D pin retirement: retire-both (lead pre-confirmed). Both #665 + #676 pins explicitly self-identify as STALE-on-fix. Lessons-learned go in PR description body / squash-commit message, not in CLAUDE.md replacement pin. Per 'no backward compat for PACT' feedback memory.

Pin retirement sequencing: AFTER squash-merge — the merge command itself uses --body-file per the #665 pin we are retiring (workaround is operationally relevant up to and including the merge action). Orchestrator-applied on main repo path /Users/mj/Sites/collab/PACT-prompt/ (CLAUDE.md gitignored, not in worktree). Confirmed pin #674 at L113 is unrelated and must NOT be touched.

Atomic commit sequence: 6 commits final (revised from 7 in §11 risk). Combined source-rename + fixture-rename into commit 1 to preserve independent greenness — splitting them would leave commit 1 with red tests until commit 3 lands. Sequence: (1) merge_guard_post field rename + 67 fixture sites + per-test cause-of-no-token assertions, (2) docstring drift sweep across 4 PostToolUse hooks, (3) task_lifecycle_gate.py:315 explanatory comment, (4) merge_guard_pre.py regex tighten + #665 tests, (5) git_commit_check.py check_planning_artifacts + tests, (6) version bump 4.1.7→4.1.8 across 4 files. NO Closes/Refs footers in individual commit messages — PR description body carries Closes #665, #676 + Refs #677 (becomes squash-commit message via gh pr merge --squash).

Version bump: patch 4.1.7 → 4.1.8. Per feedback_version_bump_semver memory. Three bug fixes + two refinements + one small extension to existing capability = patch territory. No new agent/command/protocol = not minor.

Test-fixture rename (preparer Item 2 MEDIUM uncertainty): per-test review pass REQUIRED. Every renamed test_tool_output_* test must add at least one assertion distinguishing 'early exit due to malformed tool_response' from 'no processing occurred' (option a: assert on suppress-output marker, option b: assert on _cleanup_consumed_tokens called only on non-early-exit path). Also: rename test method names bijectively test_tool_output_* → test_tool_response_* (required, not optional — honors SSOT discipline; same shape as docstring drift sweep).

task_lifecycle_gate.py:315 defensive fallback: PRESERVE line unchanged + add 4-line explanatory comment immediately above (load-bearing artifact prevents future cleanup-PR removal). Wording specified verbatim in §2.4.
handoff.reasoning_chain: Option (b) was empirically validated by extending the preparer's probe table (/tmp/regex_probe_677.py) with 4 additional edge cases including anti-evasion (heredoc body with 'gh pr merge 999' literal) and trailing-positional-digit cases. Both Option (b) and Option 4 (token-walker) produced identical probe results across 16 cases. Decision criterion was minimum-surface diff vs cleaner-architecture: Option (b) is 5 lines, Option 4 is ~25 lines + value-flag whitelist. The semantic of '_token_matches_command returns True on ambiguous extraction (None capture)' means any unsolvable edge case (multi-value-flag soup with PR positional after value-flags) degrades to 'permissive → allow' — the SAFE direction. Option 4 has identical degradation. Therefore minimum-surface wins. → Q-A scope was lead-confirmed in Task #10 acceptance; my pre-teachback prior was 'hybrid' but lead chose 'all extension surface' which matches the audit anchor's broad wording ('zero hits in source files') better. → Q-B was straightforward: enforcement style consistency with sibling security checks (check_security, check_hardcoded_secrets both exit 2). → Q-C 'no AST' decision was driven by the fact that introducing AST would create a multi-language complexity (would need separate parsers for .py/.md/.yaml/.json/.txt) for a check that fundamentally is content-categorical per CLAUDE.md wording. → Suppress-mechanism via negative-lookaround boundaries works because every legitimate exception case (Python identifier, RFC ref, version string in CHANGELOG) is structurally distinguishable from a planning-artifact identifier — F1_threshold has alphanumeric on both sides, F1 alone does not. Edge-test enumeration in §6.6 verifies. → Commit-sequence revision from 7 → 6 was driven by the §11 risk audit: original split of source-rename (commit 1) and fixture-rename (commit 3) would leave intermediate commits red. Greenness wins over diff-readability. The docstring sweep can stay as a separate commit because it has zero behavioral effect. → Pin retirement scheduling AFTER merge accounts for the merge command itself needing --body-file per the pin being retired (chicken-and-egg: pin describes workaround for a bug; merge uses workaround; merge fixes bug; THEN remove pin).

uncertainty.description: Option (b) regex returns None on multi-value-flag commands like `gh pr merge --subject 'long text' --body-file /tmp/n.md 663` (PR positional after value-flags). Acceptable degradation — _token_matches_command returns True on ambiguous (no PR-number extracted), command falls through to 'no specific context to validate against' branch which is permissive. is_dangerous_command still triggers gating. But this means PR-number consistency check is silently skipped on this command shape. If we want strict positive extraction in this case, Option 4 (token-walker with value-flag whitelist) would be needed — defer to follow-up if observed in practice.

Category B path-exclusion list (docs/, CHANGELOG.md) may be too narrow if downstream consumer repos place planning-doc paths outside docs/. Suppress-marker mechanism deferred. Re-evaluate if false positives accumulate in dogfood usage.

Independent-commit greenness for the test-fixture rename: the per-test cause-of-no-token assertion additions (§2.3) ride with commit 1. If the assertion logic introduces a test-suite dependency on internal hook details (e.g., a private function name), refactoring those internals in a future PR would break the test. Recommend assertion targets the public observable behavior (stderr suppress-output marker presence) rather than private implementation details.

Release-notes generation at merge-time still requires --body-file workaround for #665 (the regex fix lands AT the merge, not before). After this PR merges, the workaround is no longer needed for any future merge. Documented in §7.4.

integration: CODE input: this architecture doc + preparer doc. Coder must implement 6 atomic commits per §4 final sequence (revised in §11 to 6 not 7). Each commit must pass `pytest pact-plugin/tests/` independently. Specific surfaces: §2 (#676 fix + fixtures), §3 (#665 regex), §6 (Category B), §10 (version bump). Acceptance criteria for CODE teachback in §12.

TEST input: §2.3 per-test cause-of-no-token assertion requirement (MEDIUM risk mitigation), §3.4 anti-evasion test (heredoc with fake gh-pr-merge inside), §6.6 boundary edge tests for negative-lookaround word boundaries, all 16 probe cases from /tmp/regex_probe_677_arch.py.

ORCHESTRATOR input post-merge: (a) tag v4.1.8 + GitHub release per §7.3-7.4 (use --body-file / --notes-file due to #665 workaround at merge-time), (b) CLAUDE.md pin retirement per §9 — delete L82-89 (#665 pin block) + L91-110 (#676 pin block) including <!-- pinned: ... --> HTML comments; do NOT touch L113 (unrelated #674 pin).
open_questions: Confirm orchestrator will apply CLAUDE.md pin retirement post-merge (worktree is gitignored-blind to CLAUDE.md). Documented in §9 with specific line ranges + HTML-comment inclusion. Recommend lead notes this in PR merge checkpoint.

If TEST phase finds the multi-value-flag PR-positional case (Option (b) returns None) is observed in real usage, escalate to a follow-up PR introducing Option 4 token-walker. Currently treated as acceptable degradation per §3.3 + §11 risk LOW.

Suppress-marker convention (e.g., `# pact:allow-planning-artifact`) is deferred per preparer LOW uncertainty — re-evaluate after dogfood usage if false positives accumulate. No action needed in this PR.
